LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS NATIONWIDE LEGAL, LLC AND NATIONWIDE LEGAL, INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO STAY LITIGATION OR ALTERNATIVELY, TO STRIKE ALLEGATIONS IN FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS TO THE MOTION TO STAY LITIGATION, AND GRANTED WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE MOTION TO STRIKE. THE INSTANT ACTION AND THE FEDERAL ACTION DO NOT COVER THE SAME SUBJECT MATTER AS EACH PLAINTIFF IS CLASSIFIED DIFFERENTLY AS NON-EXEMPT AND SALARIED EMPLOYEES RESPECTIVELY. (CAIAFA PROF. LAW CORP. V. STATE FARM FIRE & CAS. CO. (1993) 15 CAL.APP.4TH 800, 804.) PLAINTIFF DOES NOT OPPOSE THE MOTION TO STRIKE AND REQUESTS LEAVE TO AMEND HIS ALLEGATIONS. (OPP., P. 7:20 - 8:15). DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS UNOPPOSED, AND IS THUS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S SUPPLEMENTAL REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE WAS FILED WITH ITS REPLY, AND IS DENIED AS IMPROPER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

Notice Of Motion To Stay Litigation Or Alternatively, To Strike Allegations In First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RICHARD NELSON OF THE LERNER VEIT & STANALAND, TEL. NO. 781-4000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ERIN M. SMITH & ATTY. MICHELLE M. LA MAR OF LOEB & LOEB LLP, TEL. NO. (310) 282-2113 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS NATIONWIDE LEGAL, LLC AND NATIONWIDE LEGAL, INCORPORATED'S motion To Stay Litigation Or Alternatively, To Strike Allegations In First Amended Complaint is Denied as to the motion to stay litigation, and granted with 15 days leave to amend, as to the motion to strike. The instant action and the federal action do not cover the same subject matter as each plaintiff is classified differently as non-exempt and salaried employees respectively. (Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. Co. (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800, 804.) Plaintiff does not oppose the motion to strike and requests leave to amend his allegations. (Opp., p. 7:20 - 8:15). Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is unopposed, and is thus granted. Defendant's Supplemental Request for Judicial Notice was filed with its reply, and is denied as improper.
ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =302/EG

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS NATIONWIDE LEGAL, LLC AND NATIONWIDE LEGAL, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR per the request of the moving party. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976
